title: More Money for Families with Kids
summary: In the UK, the government is giving more money to families who receive benefits. A rule that stopped parents from getting extra money for more than two children has ended. This means about 480,000 families with three or more kids will get about £4,100 more each year. A mom named Tracey, who has five children, says this extra money helps her pay for food and other things because prices are going up. She works full-time but still struggles. The change happens automatically, so parents don't need to apply. Other benefits, like for people with disabilities, are also increasing by 3.8% to match higher prices. Some people think the money could be used better elsewhere, but many families are happy.

Only for the first two kids

background_read: In the UK, the government provides benefits to help people with costs like housing, food, and children. Universal credit is a common benefit. For years, a rule called the two-child cap limited extra money to only the first two children in a family. This made it hard for larger families, especially with rising prices. Now, the cap has ended, giving more support to those families. Benefits also help people with disabilities, and these are increasing too. Understanding this helps see how government policies affect everyday life.
Article_Structure: WHO: Families in the UK, especially those with three or more children and people with disabilities.
WHAT: The government is increasing benefits, ending the two-child cap to give more money to larger families, and raising disability benefits by 3.8%.
WHY: To help families cope with the rising cost of living and support those in need, though some critics disagree.

perspectives.description: Families and charities say the extra money is a big help with living costs and improves life for many.
Some people think the government could use the money in other ways, like for different programs or savings.
